question: Identify the person shown in the portrait who started the Brahmo Samaj movement.
answer: Raja Ram Mohan Roy (1772–1833) is known as the 'Father of Indian Renaissance'. He founded the Brahmo Samaj in 1828 and fought against social evils like Sati.
caption: Raja Ram Mohan Roy, Ishwar Chandra Vidyasagar and Swami Vivekananda — socio-religious reformers

question: Which famous monument is shown in the picture that was built to commemorate the visit of King George V and Queen Mary?
answer: The Gateway of India in Mumbai was built in 1924 to welcome King George V and Queen Mary during their visit to India in 1911.

question: The map shows the extent of which empire just before the Battle of Plassey?
answer: This map shows the Mughal Empire's territory in 1765, just before the British East India Company began expanding its control after the Battle of Plassey (1757).
caption: End of the Dual Government (1772) — map of Bengal under Company rule

question: Name the leader whose statue/photo is often shown holding a spinning wheel (charkha).
answer: Mahatma Gandhi promoted the charkha (spinning wheel) as a symbol of self-reliance and Swadeshi during the freedom struggle.
caption: Non-cooperation Movement — the charkha became its symbol

question: Which picture depicts the Jallianwala Bagh massacre site?
answer: On 13 April 1919, British troops under General Dyer fired on an unarmed crowd at Jallianwala Bagh in Amritsar, killing hundreds. This incident shocked the nation.
caption: Jallianwala Bagh Massacre

question: Identify the reformer shown in the picture who fought against Sati and founded the Brahmo Samaj.
answer: Raja Ram Mohan Roy led the movement against Sati and other social evils. His efforts led to the banning of Sati in 1829.
caption: A painting showing sati or widow-burning — the evil Roy fought against

question: The painting shows Rani Lakshmibai of which kingdom during the 1857 Revolt?
answer: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi was one of the most prominent leaders of the Revolt of 1857. She fought bravely against the British.
caption: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi

question: Which map highlights the areas affected by the Indigo Revolt?
answer: The Indigo Revolt (1859–60) took place mainly in Bengal where peasants refused to grow indigo under oppressive British policies.

question: Name the Viceroy whose photo is shown in relation to the Partition of Bengal (1905).
answer: Lord Curzon partitioned Bengal in 1905, officially for administrative reasons, but it was seen as a 'divide and rule' policy.
caption: Lord Curzon

question: The picture shows the Cellular Jail located in which place?
answer: The Cellular Jail (Kala Pani) in the Andaman Islands was used by the British to exile freedom fighters like Veer Savarkar.

question: Identify the sepoy shown in the picture who is considered to have started the Revolt of 1857.
answer: Mangal Pandey refused to use the greased cartridges and attacked British officers in Barrackpore in March 1857.

question: Which building shown in the picture was Mahatma Gandhi's residence in Mumbai?
answer: Mani Bhavan in Mumbai served as Gandhi's headquarters from 1917 to 1934. It is now a museum.

question: Name the social reformer who founded the Arya Samaj.
answer: Swami Dayanand Saraswati founded the Arya Samaj in 1875. He gave the slogan "Go back to the Vedas".
caption: Dayanand Saraswati

question: The map shows the extent of which empire in the 18th century?
answer: This map shows the declining Mughal Empire and the rise of regional powers like the Marathas and Sikhs.
caption: Decline of the Mughal Empire

question: Identify the leader who gave the slogan "Swaraj is my birthright and I shall have it".
answer: Bal Gangadhar Tilak was a prominent Extremist leader. He used the press and public festivals to spread nationalism.
caption: Lal-Bal-Pal — Lala Lajpat Rai, Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Bipin Chandra Pal

question: Which monument is shown in the picture built in memory of Queen Victoria?
answer: The Victoria Memorial in Kolkata was built between 1906 and 1921. It now houses a museum of British colonial history.
caption: Queen Victoria — the monarch after whom the Victoria Memorial is named

question: Which freedom fighter was imprisoned in the Cellular Jail shown in the picture?
answer: Veer Savarkar was imprisoned in the Cellular Jail (Kala Pani). Many revolutionaries were sent to this jail during the freedom struggle.

question: Identify the historic event shown in the picture led by Mahatma Gandhi.
answer: The Dandi March (Salt March) of 1930 was a major event of the Civil Disobedience Movement against the salt tax.
caption: Dandi March (Salt Satyagraha)

question: Name the leader shown in the picture who formed the Indian National Army (INA).
answer: Subhas Chandra Bose formed the INA in 1943 with Japanese help. His slogan was "Give me blood and I will give you freedom".
caption: Subhas Chandra Bose

question: Which monument in Delhi is shown in the picture built to honour soldiers who died in World War I?
answer: India Gate was built in 1931. It also commemorates soldiers of the 1971 war.

question: Identify the first Prime Minister of India shown in the picture.
answer: Jawaharlal Nehru took oath as the first Prime Minister of independent India on 15 August 1947.
caption: Jawaharlal Nehru

question: The map shows the rise of which regional power in the 18th century?
answer: The Maratha Empire under leaders like Shivaji and later Peshwas became a major power after the decline of the Mughals.
caption: Indian camp scene during the Third Anglo-Maratha War

question: Which social reformer is shown in the picture who represented India at the World Parliament of Religions in 1893?
answer: Swami Vivekananda founded the Ramakrishna Mission and spread the message of Vedanta in the West.
caption: Swami Vivekananda

question: The map shows the division of which province in 1905?
answer: Bengal was partitioned by Lord Curzon into East and West Bengal, leading to the Swadeshi Movement.
caption: Lord Curzon — responsible for the Partition of Bengal (1905)

question: Which incident is shown in the picture that led to the withdrawal of the Non-Cooperation Movement?
answer: In 1922, a violent mob burnt a police station in Chauri Chaura, killing 22 policemen. Gandhi called off the movement.

question: Identify the leader shown in the picture who died after being lathi-charged during the Simon Commission protest.
answer: Lala Lajpat Rai, known as 'Punjab Kesari', died in 1928 due to injuries sustained during a protest against the Simon Commission.
caption: Lal-Bal-Pal — includes Lala Lajpat Rai

question: From which historic monument did the Prime Minister of India deliver his Independence Day speech?
answer: The Red Fort in Delhi has been the site of the Prime Minister's Independence Day address since 1947.

question: Identify the social reformer shown in the picture who worked for the education of lower castes and women.
answer: Jyotirao Phule founded the Satyashodhak Samaj and opened schools for girls and Dalits in Maharashtra.
caption: 19th-century social reform movements in India — visual mapping

question: Name the leader shown in the picture who is known as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'.
answer: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee of the Indian Constitution.
caption: B.R. Ambedkar

question: Which movement is shown in the picture that was launched by Gandhi in 1942?
answer: The Quit India Movement demanded immediate end to British rule. Gandhi gave the slogan "Do or Die".
caption: Mahatma Gandhi and the Indian National Movement: 1917–1947

question: The picture shows the split in which political party in 1907?
answer: The Indian National Congress split into Moderates and Extremists at the Surat session in 1907.
caption: The Rise of Indian Nationalism — review exercise

question: Identify the Moderate leader shown in the picture who was the political guru of Mahatma Gandhi.
answer: Gopal Krishna Gokhale believed in constitutional methods and was a mentor to Gandhi.
caption: Gopal Krishna Gokhale

question: Which historic building shown in the picture was used to detain Mahatma Gandhi during the Quit India Movement?
answer: The Aga Khan Palace in Pune is where Gandhi was imprisoned. Kasturba Gandhi and Mahadev Desai died here.

question: Name the ashram shown in the picture where Mahatma Gandhi lived from 1936 onwards.
answer: Sevagram Ashram in Wardha, Maharashtra, became Gandhi's main residence during the later years of the freedom struggle.

question: Identify the ruler shown in the picture who fought against the British in the Anglo-Mysore Wars.
answer: Tipu Sultan, the 'Tiger of Mysore', resisted British expansion until his death in 1799.
caption: Tipu Sultan

question: Name the leader shown in the picture who started the Home Rule Movement in India.
answer: Annie Besant launched the Home Rule League in 1916 demanding self-government for India.
caption: Annie Besant

question: Which protest movement is shown in the picture against the Rowlatt Act?
answer: The Rowlatt Act (1919) gave the British government powers to arrest people without trial, leading to nationwide protests and the Jallianwala Bagh massacre.
caption: Jallianwala Bagh — protest against the Rowlatt Act

question: Identify the Governor-General shown in the picture who introduced the Doctrine of Lapse.
answer: Lord Dalhousie used the Doctrine of Lapse to annex many Indian states, which became a major cause of the 1857 Revolt.
caption: Expansion of British Power in India — Lord Dalhousie and the Doctrine of Lapse

question: The picture shows the first session of which organisation held in 1885?
answer: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885. Its first session was held in Bombay under W.C. Bonnerjee.
caption: Formation of the Indian National Congress in 1885

question: Which protest is shown in the picture against the all-British commission?
answer: The Simon Commission (1927) was boycotted because it had no Indian members. People protested with black flags.

question: The map shows the partition of which country in 1947?
answer: India was partitioned into India and Pakistan on 15 August 1947 under the Mountbatten Plan.
caption: Events leading to Partition of India (1947)

question: Identify the leader shown in the picture known as the 'Iron Man of India'.
answer: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel played a key role in integrating 565 princely states into the Indian Union.
caption: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel

question: Which plan is shown in the picture that proposed a united India with a weak centre?
answer: The Cabinet Mission Plan of 1946 was the last attempt by the British to keep India united before partition.
caption: Cabinet Mission Plan (1946)

question: The picture shows the trial of officers of which army?
answer: The INA trials at the Red Fort in 1945–46 created massive public support for the independence movement.
caption: Netaji and members of the Azad Hind Fauj (INA)

question: Which movement is associated with the leader shown in the picture?
answer: Annie Besant started the Home Rule Movement demanding self-rule for India during World War I.
caption: Annie Besant

question: The picture shows the first hoisting of the Indian national flag at which place on 15 August 1947?
answer: Jawaharlal Nehru hoisted the tricolour at the Red Fort on 15 August 1947.
caption: Jawaharlal Nehru — first Prime Minister of independent India

question: The map shows the plan prepared by which last Viceroy of India?
answer: Lord Mountbatten announced the plan for the partition of India in June 1947.
caption: Lord Mountbatten — last Viceroy of India

question: Identify the first President of India shown in the picture.
answer: Dr. Rajendra Prasad was the first President of independent India from 1950 to 1962.
caption: Dr. Rajendra Prasad

question: The picture shows the headquarters of which international organisation?
answer: The United Nations was formed in 1945. India was one of the founding members.
caption: The United Nations

question: The picture shows the adoption of which important document on 26 November 1949?
answer: The Constitution of India was adopted by the Constituent Assembly on 26 November 1949 and came into force on 26 January 1950.
caption: Organs of the Indian Government — from the Constitution chapter
